title: carex complanata subsp. hirsutella
intro: Carex complanata subsp. hirsutella, also known as the hairy sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex complanata plant, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial, aquatic plant that grows in wetlands and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Carex complanata subsp. hirsutella can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
